pr.title: feat: encode harness in chip icon, surface model in chip text
pr.body:
## Why

Long-term follow-up to #14505. The conversation chip used to communicate **either** the harness brand **or** the LLM model, never both. This PR surfaces both signals without spending any extra horizontal width: the **icon** encodes the harness, the **text** encodes the model.

| Conversation                          | Icon          | Text                | Tooltip                                  |
|---------------------------------------|---------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| OpenHands + `anthropic/sonnet-4-5-…`  | Circuit       | `Claude Sonnet 4.5` | `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`   |
| Claude Code (ACP) + `opus-4-1`        | Puzzle piece  | `Claude Opus 4.1`   | `Claude Code · anthropic/claude-opus-4-1`|
| Claude Code (ACP) — no model exposed  | Puzzle piece  | `Claude Code`       | `Claude Code`                            |
| Codex (ACP) + `gpt-5`                 | Puzzle piece  | `GPT-5`             | `Codex · openai/gpt-5`                   |

## Summary

- New util `formatLlmModel(raw)` strips routing prefixes (incl. chained `litellm_proxy/anthropic/…`), drops date suffixes, prettifies Claude / GPT / o-series / Gemini families; unknown models fall through with the prefix stripped.
- `agentDisplayLabel` renamed to `resolveAgentChip` and returns `{ kind, text, tooltip }` so each call site picks the icon by kind without re-deriving from raw fields.
- New shared `AgentChipIcon` component — circuit for OpenHands, puzzle piece for ACP. Both icons already in the repo, no new assets needed.
- Three call sites updated: sidebar conversation card, home Recent Conversations, conversation header. Layout untouched.

Explicit non-goals: per-provider ACP brand marks (Claude Code / Codex / Gemini logos) and a two-chip layout. Punted for now to keep the diff small and the sidebar width intact.

## Issue Number

None — long-term follow-up to PR #14505 (no GitHub issue tracks this).

## How to Test

Locally:

```bash
# backend (native, no docker)
ENABLE_ACP=true poetry run python -m uvicorn openhands.server.listen:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000
# frontend
cd frontend && VITE_BACKEND_HOST=127.0.0.1:3000 npm run dev
```

1. Open an OpenHands native conversation with an Anthropic model → chip should show the prettified model name (e.g. `Claude Sonnet 4.5`) next to a circuit icon; hover the chip and the raw `anthropic/…` string appears.
2. Open an ACP (Claude Code) conversation without an underlying `llm_model` → chip shows `Claude Code` next to a puzzle-piece icon; hover repeats `Claude Code`.
3. Open an ACP conversation where `llm_model` is set → chip shows the prettified model name next to the puzzle-piece icon; hover shows `<Provider> · <raw model>`.

Unit tests:

```bash
cd frontend && npm test -- --run \
  __tests__/utils/format-llm-model.test.ts \
  __tests__/utils/agent-display-label.test.ts \
  __tests__/components/features/home/recent-conversation.test.tsx \
  __tests__/components/features/conversation/conversation-name.test.tsx \
  __tests__/components/features/conversation-panel/conversation-card.test.tsx
# 89 passed
```

## Video/Screenshots

Not attached. Visual behavior is fully exercised by the component tests under `frontend/__tests__/components/features/{home,conversation,conversation-panel}/…` — each asserts the prettified text, the tooltip, and the icon presence for every code path (OpenHands+model, ACP+no-model, ACP+model). A human reviewer is welcome to validate locally via the steps in **How to Test**.

## Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Docs / chore

## Notes

- `agentDisplayLabel` is gone — call sites now use `resolveAgentChip`. No external consumers, so no shim.
- Per-provider ACP brand marks (Claude Code / Codex / Gemini logos) are a deliberate follow-up; the generic puzzle piece communicates "external pluggable agent" cleanly enough for v1.
- Backwards compat: existing testids preserved (`*-llm-model`). Tooltip semantics change — the sidebar tooltip used to show the harness brand and now shows the raw provider/model; this is a side benefit, not a regression.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---

To run this PR locally, use the following command:

GUI with Docker:
```
docker run -it --rm   -p 3000:3000   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock   --add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway   --name openhands-app-79579c6   docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:79579c6
```
pr.author: simonrosenberg
